science.title: SF Campaign: Why do people living at high altitudes have lower rates of diabetes?
science.description: Sherpas, for instance, carry genetic variants that suppress the usual altitude-driven increase in red blood cells, and unlike most other mountain communities, they do not enjoy improved blood sugar control. This fits perfectly with the new mechanism.
